content.text:
{
  "dimension": "intervention-quality",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach uses AoA-style moves (reflecting client's exact language, asking open questions, noticing what closes down) and avoids the major traps — they don't argue the fearlessness, don't push the client to admit fear, don't teach or evangelize, don't diagnose dissociation. The questions are tight and well-paced. However, the move selection stays in one register: repeatedly probing the fear angle through cognitive/conceptual questions ('what if it were fear?', 'what would you be dealing with?', 'what if it does [bother you]?'). The coach keeps trying to get fear to surface via thinking, when the expected territory (and the client's own signals: 'it doesn't really land anywhere,' 'nothing behind it,' 'I don't really feel anything,' 'coming up empty') is pointing directly at the numbness/'fine' layer. The pivot to body and to the texture of 'fine' never happens. Turn 14 finally edges toward it ('where are you checking?') which is a better-matched move, but it took six turns of similar probes to get there. The right move (meet-resistance-with-okay, ask about the felt quality of 'fine,' move to body) was available repeatedly and missed. Hit rate roughly 50%: good restraint, good mirroring, but the move sequence skipped the felt layer the concern requires.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Notices the door closing and reflects it back without arguing; opens a hypothetical that doesn't push. Reasonable opening move."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Mirrors client's exact phrase ('I'd deal with it') — good AoA technique — but the follow-up keeps the frame on fear-as-content rather than tracking the felt texture."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Picks up client's language ('sit with the complexity') and asks about content. Misses opportunity to ask what 'sitting with' actually feels like."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Good mirror, but keeps the inquiry in the cognitive/analytical domain when the client has already given multiple signals of flatness."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client just dissociated mid-thought ('it was there and then it wasn't'). Coach pushes for the lost content rather than meeting the disappearance itself — misses 'disassociating-in-the-coaching-room' read."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client says 'I don't really feel anything in particular' and 'feel pretty fine.' Coach doubles down on the fear hypothesis ('what if it does [bother you]?') instead of pivoting to the texture of 'fine.' Repeats same kind of move that has been missing."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Finally a better-matched move — 'where are you checking?' begins pointing toward the felt/body layer. Right move, but arriving late in the arc."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["meet-resistance-with-okay", "the-happiness-filter", "disassociating-in-the-coaching-room", "how-is-my-anxiety-right-now", "fear-in-body-vs-fear-in-head"],
  "notes": "Coach avoids all listed anti-patterns (no arguing, no pushing, no validating, no diagnosing, no teaching) — strong restraint. But the repertoire stays narrow: variations of 'what if it's fear?' delivered cognitively. The numbness layer / quality-of-'fine' move never deployed until the final turn, which is the heart of what this concern requires."
}
